question: Does down-weighting COMMIT tier at N>=3 concurrency reduce wasted work?
expect: COMMIT tier score drops from 100 to <=50 at N>=3 concurrency. Priority drops from P_COMMIT(0) to P_PERIODIC(5).
actual: CONFIRMED. At N=5 detected concurrency: score 100->40 (-60%), priority 0->5. COMMIT items now rank below DUE/CLOSE/STRATEGY/DISPATCH. At N<3, behavior unchanged.
diff: Exact match on scoring. Priority-tier shift (P_COMMIT->P_PERIODIC) more impactful than score reduction: re-orders COMMIT below all work tiers. Proxy absorption handles artifact commits; sessions focus on productive work.
external: Queuing theory: at high arrival rate, tasks with near-zero marginal value should be deprioritized
falsified_if: At N>=3, proxy absorption rate drops below 80% for untracked files
